content.problemStatement:
A string is called a **happy prefix** if it is a non-empty prefix which is also a suffix of the string (excluding the string itself).

Given a string s, your task is to find the **longest happy prefix** of sss. If no such prefix exists, print an empty string.

Function DeclarationFunction NamelongestHappyPrefixlongestHappyPrefixlongestHappyPrefix – This function finds the longest prefix of a string that is also its suffix.

Parameters
sss : A string consisting of lowercase English letters.
Return Value
Returns a string — the **longest happy prefix**.
Returns an **empty string** if no happy prefix exists.
Constraints
1≤T≤1001 \leq T \leq 1001≤T≤100
1≤∣s∣≤1051 \leq |s| \leq 10^51≤∣s∣≤105
sss contains only lowercase English letters

Input Format
The first line contains an integer TTT — the number of test cases.


Each of the next TTT lines contains a string sss consisting of lowercase English letters.



Output Format

For each test case, print a single line containing the **longest happy prefix**.
If no such prefix exists, print an **empty line**.
constraints: s contains only lowercase English letters
content.inputFormat: The first line contains an integer T (1 ≤ T ≤ 100) – the number of test cases. Each of the next T lines contains a non‑empty string s consisting only of lowercase English letters (1 ≤ |s| ≤ 10^5).
content.outputFormat: For each test case output a single line containing the longest happy prefix of s. If no such prefix exists, output an empty line.

sampleTestCases.explanation: Test case 1: "ababab" has "abab" as the longest prefix that is also a suffix.\nTest case 2: "aaaa" has "aaa" as the longest happy prefix.\nTest case 3: "abcd" has no non‑empty border, so an empty line is printed.
For "abcababcab" the longest border is "abcab".\nFor "zzzzzz" the longest border is five "z" characters.
A single character string has no proper prefix that is also a suffix, so the answer is empty.

solution.approach: For each string we compute the prefix function (also called the failure function) used in the KMP algorithm. The value of the prefix function at the last position gives the length of the longest proper prefix which is also a suffix – exactly the longest happy prefix. The algorithm scans the string once, updating the current matched length using previously computed values, achieving O(|s|) time and O(|s|) auxiliary space.
solution.timeComplexity: O(|s|) per test case, total O(∑|s|) over all test cases.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(|s|) auxiliary array for the prefix function.
